channel.title: dotnet/aspnetcore Pull Requests
channel.description: Pull Requests from https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore, generated by https://github.com/meziantou/IssuesToRss/

item.title: PR: Observe changes to IWebHostBuilder settings - @davidfowl
item.description:
• Today we have a complex process to bootstrap configuration, then we use that configuration to bootstrap the default services. This process missed configuration updates in some narrow cases, and one of those was caught by the IIS out of process extension method.
  • Today when we configure the WebApplicationBuilder, we end up not catching all calls to UseSetting on the various IWebHostBuilder implementations that are created during bootstrapping. This was observable in 2 ways:
    1. Calls to WebApplicationBuilder.WebHost.UseSetting
    2. Any call to UseSetting during the DeferredHostBuilder. They happen for the same reason, that is, calls to UseSetting tried to mutate the final GenericWebHostBuilder via ApplySettings, and that configuration source gets thrown away (we clear them all assuming they were already applied). The fix implemented here uses the ConfigurationManager as the source of truth in all cases. We use it as the backing store for the DeferredWebHostBuilder and we add the GenericWebHostBuilder's configuration sources to it. That way it should truly see all of the configuration values.

The reason the IIS case found this issue is because the configuration bootstrapping process misses configuration values added during callbacks to configure services (and possibly other callbacks as well).

PS: I can't really test the IIS case in a unit test because the repro relies on code that runs inside of the call to WebApplication.CreateBuilder

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/issues/34565

item.title: PR: Exact subject match for CertificateLoader - @vstr
item.description:
• [x] You've read the Contributor Guide and Code of Conduct.
  • [x] You've included unit or integration tests for your change, where applicable.
  • [x] You've included inline docs for your change, where applicable.
  • [x] There's an open issue for the PR that you are making. If you'd like to propose a new feature or change, please open an issue to discuss the change or find an existing issue.

PR Title Enable exact subject match for CertificateLoader.LoadFromStoreCert

PR Description Try to match on exact subject when looking for certificate, if no match, fallback to substring default implementation.

Addresses #33070

item.title: PR: Remove async state machines from middlewares when they do nothing - @campersau
item.description:
PR Title Remove async state machines from middlewares when they do nothing

PR Description Searched for await _next( and found a couple more in src\Http and src\Middleware

  • MapMiddleware
  • MapWhenMiddleware
  • UsePathBaseMiddleware
  • MigrationsEndPointMiddleware
  • HttpMethodOverrideMiddleware
  • ResponseCompressionMiddleware
  • SpaProxyMiddleware
  • ConditionalProxyMiddleware
  • IISMiddleware

Addresses https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/issues/34574

item.title: PR: Fix Host/WebHost doc comment mix-up - @Eilon
item.description: Just doc comment fix-ups.

item.title: PR: HTTP/3: Http3Stream pooling - @JamesNK
item.description:
This builds on top of the new IPersistentStateFeature.

Http3Stream and many of its associated types (pipes, output producer, decoder) are now reused between requests.

In this PR I also added some basic microbenchmarks that do HTTP/3. Refactoring the test code so it is usable in microbenchmarks is the major of line changes in this PR.

I will get benchmarks comparing allocations before and after.

item.title: PR: Update license from Apache to MIT - @Pilchie
item.description:
Fixes #18873.

Most work done by either Find and Replace or the Visual Studio fix to add missing headers.

item.title: PR: Remove mention about SPA project templates - @mkArtakMSFT
item.description: We've moved the SPA projects into the dotnet/spa-templates repo, but missed to update the readme.

item.title: PR: Add Latin1 header support to HttpSys - @wtgodbe
item.description:
Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/issues/34330

Similar to https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/pull/22798/

item.title: PR: Skip, quarantine and un-Quarantine - @dougbu
item.description:
• un-quarantine ContentLength_Received_MultipleDataFramesOverSize_Reset, #33373 fix seems to have worked
  • quarantine LoggingConnectionMiddlewareCanBeAddedBeforeAndAfterHttps(), #34561
  • skip LongPollingTransportOnReceiveGetsCalled() because we can't quarantine Java tests, #34563

item.title: PR: Problem details cleanup - @pranavkm
item.description:
• Move HttpValidationProblemDetails to Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http per API review
  • Return Content-Type headers when returning ProblemDetails responses

Fixes #34509

item.title: PR: Require Win10 for ALPN tests - @Tratcher
item.description: There are new tests that failed on Win8.1 because HTTP/2 isn't supported there. https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/pull/34242

item.title: PR: Skip running some templates on ARM - @BrennanConroy
item.description:
https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/issues/34554

Removed the OSX skip because the issue it was linked to has been closed.

item.title: PR: WASM: popup readme for identity server - @HaoK
item.description:
Part of https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/issues/32881

Show a readme text when identity server is used to highlight its license (hosted only + individual auth for blazor wasm)

item.title: PR: Update SDK - @BrennanConroy
PR: HTTP/3: Clone multiplex middleware with listener - @JamesNK
item.description: Multiplex middleware wasn't being copied when ListenOptions were cloned.

item.title: PR: Added MapFallback overloads that take Delegate - @davidfowl
item.description:
• These new APIs are enlightened with the new RequestDelegateFactory and get the binding and results features that were added in .NET 6.
  • Added some basic tests

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/issues/34450

item.title: PR: Add comment about order of baggage items - @shirhatti
item.description: Follow up from https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/pull/33777

item.title: PR: Stop retrying with project fixtures with selenium e2e - @HaoK
item.description:
The retry attribute is retrying tests directly without going through any test initialization so it doesn't work with the fixtures in components e2e that require reinitialization, so removing the attribute from this assembly

cc @SteveSandersonMS @TanayParikh

item.title: PR: Add a transport package for Razor Source Generator - @pranavkm
item.description: Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/issues/33536

item.title: PR: Blazor <head> modification using a section-based approach (preview7) - @MackinnonBuck
item.description:
Description

This PR introduces a way for developers to modify the HTML <head> contents from Blazor.

Customer Impact

With this feature, customers will be able to easily

  • Modify the document title (tab name).
  • Add link previews for links to their web app.
  • Optimize their website for SEO.
  • Dynamically change style sheets (e.g. switch between light and dark modes).

Other SPA frameworks have third-party libraries enabling <head> modification, so this change makes Blazor an even more viable alternative.

Regression?

  • [ ] Yes
  • [X] No

Risk

  • [ ] High
  • [ ] Medium
  • [X] Low

This change is almost entirely additive, so there's little risk of introducing a regression.

Verification

  • [X] Manual (required)
  • [X] Automated

Packaging changes reviewed?

  • [ ] Yes
  • [ ] No
  • [X] N/A

Addresses #25705

item.title: PR: [SignalR TS] Update msgpack dependency to latest version - @BrennanConroy
item.description: Update dependency before we ship RC1 so we're up to date.

item.title: PR: Make sync and async disposal of Renderer behave the same - @pranavkm
PR: [Static web assets] Updates hosting to use the new static web assets manifest format - @javiercn
item.description:
Description

The build now generates a new manifest in JSON format with name <<AppName>>.staticwebasets.runtime.json that contains all the mappings for static web assets. This change checks for the existence of that file and uses it in case is present in favor of the previous manifest format.

The manifest is an object that contains two descendants ContentRoots which is an ordered list of folders on disk, and Root which is a tree structure that represents the root of the "combined" static web assets "file system".

Each node of the tree has a list of Children (having children means you are a folder) and a list of Patterns (Parts of the tree where we might need to check if a file exists on disk (this is because we need to support discovering new files without regenerating the manifest)) or it can contain a Match, which indicates the content root and the path to use to retrieve the file.

We don't have to deal with conflicts (files that would end on the same path) or things like that, since the build process already takes care of making those checks.

Here is a sample manifest generated by the SDK

The PR is divided in three commits:

  • The first commit is the important one and contains the new manifest implementation and tests.
  • The second commit contains a bump of the SDK to a version high enough that contains the new MSBuild targets we need.
  • The third commit applies workarounds for SDK bugs (already fixed in another PR) and will be reverted after preview7.

Customer Impact

Customers won't be able to load Blazor apps unless they switch to the previous Static Web Assets implementation (we included a flag for this) however, we want to make sure this is the default experience in Preview7 to ensure that we uncover as many issues as we can, since these improvements are the foundation for many of the new features we are building

Regression?

  • [X] Yes
  • [ ] No

6.0 Preview6

Risk

  • [ ] High
  • [x] Medium
  • [ ] Low

The changes are complex, however we put a fallback mechanism in place to force the older implementation to be used. In case a scenario doesn't work for a user, they can resort to the preview6 implementation setting a value on their csproj file.

Verification

  • [X] Manual (required)
  • [X] Automated

Manual verification is problematic and limited due to the fact that there is a build time component and a runtime component and that they live on different repositories.

I've been able to use a freshly built "dev sdk" to build one of the samples and i've used the runtime changes (paired with this ones) to run the project and run the published project.

Packaging changes reviewed?

  • [ ] Yes
  • [ ] No
  • [ ] N/A

item.title: PR: Update spa-templates submodule - @HaoK
item.description: Result of git submodule update --remote spa-templates attempting to pickup my spa changes

item.title: PR: Support optionality via nullability and default values - @captainsafia
item.description:
• Adds codegen to validate required parameter if they are not nullable or don't have a default value
  • Allows developers to use [FromBody(AllowEmpty = true)] to override required parameters defined in method
  • Favors implicit services over body parameters when a non-string or non-parseable argument is typed as optional

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/issues/32375

item.title: PR: Update Blazor project templates to mutate the <head>. - @MackinnonBuck
item.description:
Update Blazor project templates to mutate the <head> The Blazor project templates have been update to use <PageTitle> to set the <title> depending on the current page.

Addresses #34497

item.title: PR: Correct spa-templates submodule - @dougbu
item.description:
• correct the repo URI
  • move to the current commit

item.title: PR: Update to Ms.Id.Web 1.14.1 - @jmprieur
item.description:
PR Title Update the web project templates to Microsoft.Identity.Web 1.14.1

PR Description

  • Update the version of Microsoft.Identity.Web
  • Simplify the web API controlers

item.title: PR: Issue-#34489 - cancel timer on server timeout - @vbatr
item.description:
• [x] You've read the Contributor Guide and Code of Conduct.
  • [x] You've included unit or integration tests for your change, where applicable.
  • [x] You've included inline docs for your change, where applicable.
  • [x] There's an open issue for the PR that you are making. If you'd like to propose a new feature or change, please open an issue to discuss the change or find an existing issue.

PR Description

  • original issue - https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/issues/34489
  • fix the issue with unclosed threads created by the ping timer on reconnects

#34489
